censurable.def: 該責備的、可批評的人的人物
censurable.ex: His failure to report the accident was highly censurable.

censure.def: 責難、非難、譴責、非難的人的人、非難地 (責)
censure.col: vote of censure (不信任動議), severe censure
censure.ex: The politician faced severe censure for his recent actions.

census.def: 人口普查、調查、普查的人物 (單數)
census.ex: The national census is carried out every ten years in Japan.

cent.def: 分(貨幣單位)、百的人物 (單數)
cent.col: per cent (百分之), red cent (一分錢)
cent.ex: He doesn't have a single red cent left in his bank account.

centaur.def: 半人半馬怪物的人物 (單數)
centaur.ex: In Greek mythology, the centaur is a wild and wise creature.

centenary.def: 一百週年、一百年的、百歲老人人物 (單數)
centenary.ex: The school is planning a grand centenary celebration next year.

centennial.def: 一百週年、一百年的的人物 (單數)
centennial.ex: This year marks the centennial of the end of the Great War.

center/centre.def: 中心、核心、中樞、置中、居中的人的人物、居中地 (中)
center/centre.col: town center, call center, front and center, shopping center
center/centre.ex: The fountain is located in the very center of the town park.

centered/centred.def: 中心化的、集中的的人的人 (或物)
centered/centred.ex: The nurse provides a client-centered approach to healthcare.

centering/centring.def: 置中、定中心、核心作業的人 (或物) (中)
centering/centring.ex: The new software includes an auto centering tool for images.

centesimal.def: 百分之一的、百分的的的人物 (單數)
centesimal.ex: The centesimal system of measurement is used in some fields.

centigrade.def: 攝氏、百分度的的人物 (攝氏)
centigrade.ex: The centigrade scale is commonly used to measure temperature.

centigram/centigramme.def: 毫克、百分之一克的人物 (單數)
centigram/centigramme.ex: The scale was accurate to the nearest tenth of a centigram.

centiliter/centilitre.def: 百分之一公升、厘升的人物 (單數)
centiliter/centilitre.ex: The liquid medication is measured in centiliters for kids.

centime.ex: They didn't have a single centime left after their long trip.

centimeter/centimetre.ex: The small wooden ruler is thirty centimeters long in total.

centipede.ex: A giant centipede crawled slowly across the dark garden path.

central.def: 中心的、中央的、主要的、電話總機的人物、中心地 (核心)
central.col: central heating, central bank, central nervous system
central.ex: Central heating is essential for the cold winter months here.

centrality.def: 中心、核心性、主要的地位人物 (單數)
centrality.ex: The study highlights the centrality of play in early memory.

centralization.ex: The company moved towards increased centralization of power.

centralize.def: 使集中、中央集權、集中的人的人、集中地 (集)
centralize.ex: The new CEO plans to centralize the decision making process.

centrally.def: 在中心地、主要地、集中地
centrally.ex: The economy was controlled centrally by the national union.

centre/center.def: 中心、核心、居中心的人物、置中地 (中)
centre/center.ex: The local community centre offers many free classes for kids.

centric.def: 中心的、核心的人的人物
centric.ex: The scientist proposed a new centric model of the universe.

centrifugal.ex: He felt the strong centrifugal force as the car turned fast.

centrifuge.def: 離心機、使受離心作用的人物、分離地 (分)
centrifuge.ex: The lab technician used a centrifuge to separate the blood.

centripetal.ex: Centripetal force keeps the moon in orbit around the earth.

centrist.def: 中間派的、中堅的、中間派議員的人物 (單數)
centrist.ex: The centrist politician tried to appeal to both major parties.

centroid.ex: The student learned how to calculate the centroid of a triangle.

centuple.def: 百倍的、使增至百倍、百倍的人的人物、百倍地 (百)
centuple.ex: The company aims to centuple its profits over ten long years.

centurion.ex: A Roman centurion was in charge of a group of eighty men.

century.def: 一世紀、一百年的人物 (單數)
century.col: last century, turn of the century, current century
century.ex: The castle was built back in the early thirteenth century.
